title: Quantification of timelapse 3D tumor spheroid killing activity of NK cells using a live-cell imaging system
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"eidq5","text":"The protocols for isolation of peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs), negative selection of peripheral blood NK (PB-NK) cells, expansion , and evaluation of PB-NK cells using flow cytometry are performed following our published work (1). Here, we describe the assessment of NK cell cytotoxic activities against 3D tumor spheroids, live-cell imaging utilizing EVOS M7000 imaging system, and the subsequent analysis of spheroid killing via Celleste, GraphPad Prism, and ImageJ software.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Passaging solid cancer cell lines For the 3D tumor spheroid killing assay, we use KKU-213A and MDA-MB-231, representing aggressive cancer cell lines, as targets. KKU-213A cells are cultured in the complete DMEM/F12 medium, while MDA-MB-231 cells are cultured in the complete DMEM medium. Cells are passaged when they attain 70-80% confluence, typically occurring within 3-4 days, and are cultured in a 25-cm 2 culture flask.
Remove the culture medium and rinse the vessels with 1X PBS.
Add 1 mL of 0.1% trypsin/EDTA into the flask and incubate the cells at 37 °C, 5% CO 2 for 5 min.
Centrifuge the cells at 500 × g at room temperature for 5 min.
After the incubation, add 4 mL of complete medium and thoroughly resuspend the cells using a serological pipette.
Transfer the cell suspension to a 15-mL conical tube.
Discard supernatant and resuspend the cells with 1 mL of complete medium.
Perform cell counting using the trypan blue exclusion assay.
Transfer 2 × 10 5 viable cells into a new 25-cm 2 culture flask containing 5 mL of complete medium and incubate the cells at 37 °C, 5% CO 2 .
CFSE labeling
Perform cell dissociation as described in steps 1.1 to 1.7.
Transfer 2 × 10 5 viable cells to a 15-mL conical tube, wash with 1X PBS.
Centrifuge the cells at 500 × g at room temperature for 5 min.
Discard the supernatant and resuspend the cells with 1 mL of 1X PBS.
Add CFSE solution to the final concentration of 2.5 µM, vortex briefly and incubate at 37 °C, 5% CO 2 for 20 min in the dark.
After incubation, add 5 mL of complete cancer media to the cell suspension.
Centrifuge the cells at 500 × g at room temperature for 5 min.
Discard the supernatant and resuspend the cells with 1 mL of culture media.
Formation of tumour spheroids
Prepare cancer culture media containing 2.5% Matrigel matrix and chill the media on ice.
Aliquot appropriate cell number of the CFSE-labelled cholangiocarcinoma or breast cancer cells to a 15-mL conical tube.
Centrifuge the cells at 500 × g at room temperature for 5 min.
Discard the supernatant.
Resuspend the CFSE-labelled cholangiocarcinoma or breast cancer cells with pre-chilled culture media containing 2.5% Matrigel matrix.
Transfer the CFSE-labelled cholangiocarcinoma or breast cancer cells onto an ultra-low attachment round-bottom 96-well plate at a seeding density of 2 × 10 3 cells/100 µL/well.
Centrifuge the plate at 1,000 × g for 10 min at 4 °C.
Incubate the spheroids at 37 °C, 5% CO 2 for 2 days.
Co-culture of PB-NK cells with tumour spheroids
After 2 days of spheroid formation, thaw PB-NK cells in the PB-NK cell medium.
Transfer the thawed PB-NK cells to a 15-mL tube and centrifuge at 500 × g at room temperature for 5 min.

After centrifugation, remove the supernatant, resuspend the cell pellet in 1 mL of PB-NK culture medium, and portion the necessary number of PB-NK cells for all co-culture condition into a 15-mL conical tube.
Centrifuge the cells at 500 × g at room temperature for 5 min.
Discard the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet with an appropriate volume of PB-NK culture medium without IL-2 supplementation.

Add propidium iodide (PI) to the concentration of 2 μg/mL and mix thoroughly using an autopipette We recommend performing at least 3 replicates for each E:T ratio, therefore, prepare approximately 5 mL of PB-NK medium containing 2 μg/mL PI. .

Add 100 μL of the suspended PB-NK cells to a well of the ultra-low attachment round-bottom 96-well plate containing tumour spheroids (from step 3.8) with cell numbers adjusted according to various effector-to-target (E:T) ratios; for this protocol, E:T ratios of 0:1, 1:1, 2.5:1, and 5:1, are utilised, therefore, the NK cell number should be 2 × 10 3 , 5 × 10 3 , and 1 × 10 4 cells/well for 1:1, 2.5:1, and 5:1 ratio, respectively. Carefully add the PB-NK cells to prevent disruption of the spheroids. The total volume of culture medium per well is 200 μL, with the final concentration of PI being 1 μg/mL.
Centrifuge the 96-well plate at 1,000 × g for 10 min at 4 °C.
Add 0.1% Triton X-100 to the positive control wells to assess the maximum level of death. The maximum PI intensity of the positive control could be observed within the first 6 hours of co-culture.

Incubate the cells at 37 °C, 5% CO 2 in the EVOS M7000 imaging system and record the data for three days. Prepare control wells containing only the tumour spheroids (0:1 ratio) to determine the baseline level of spontaneous tumour cell death.
Mean fluorescence intensity data acquisition using Celleste software
Collect data from 51 hours of co-culture from the EVOS M7000 imaging system.
Open the files in the Celleste program v5 by clicking ‘Open Images as Sequences’.

Select all files that include “…_z0…” and select ‘Group Related Files’ and ‘Open As Sequences’, then click ‘Open’ This process might take some time, depending on the power of the hardware. Figure 1 File selection for analysis

Click ‘Add Volume Measurement’ and select the channel for analysis, in this case, “RFP_Tiled” is used for analyzing PI intensity. The green area will appear in the interested area. This program allows analysis of several channels, including brightfield. In this protocol, we use PI and CFSE, thus we analysed only RFP and GFP channels. Figure 2 ‘Add Volume Measurements’ button for creating 3D volume measurements. Figure 3 Pop-up window after creating volume measurement.
Adjust the RFP from the last time point to maximise the area of PI intensity.
Adjust GFP from the first time point to maximise the area of CFSE intensity by selecting “GFP_Tiled” (same menu as in step 5.4). The volume of RFP and GFP should not be much different.
Click on ‘3D Measurement’, then click ‘Edit Measurement Range’

Adjust “Volumes: Volume” and “Volumes: Density (mean)” to correct the size of only spheroids, not other trace objects in the wells. Figure 4 'Edit Measurement Range' window for adjusting the volume of spheroids to be analyzed.
Adjust the range of both GFP and RFP to be the same within the same well. Figure 5 Adjustment of red and green are for analysis.
Click ‘Show Data Table’, then export the data to Microsoft Excel.

Strategy 1: Visualisation of dying position within the tumor spheroids upon co-culturing with NK cells using ImageJ software The dying position within the spheroids can be visualized to present different dying patterns of cancer cells. We use ImageJ software to analyze the fluorescent images taken from EVOS M7000. The brightfield images from 3 timepoints (first, middle, and final timepoints) are used to gate regions of interest (ROI) for analyzing the intensity profiles of PI intensity in each spheroid.
Open the brightfield image of spheroid at the final timepoint (51 hours) in the ImageJ software.
Use the ‘straight line’ tool to choose only the area of interest within a spheroid. Figure 6 Selecting 'straight line' tool in ImageJ software and gating of spheroid area using brightfield image.
Select ‘Analyse’, then choose the ROI manager to record the ROI.

Rename the ROI to be the name of the selected well and timepoint. Saving the ROI is recommended by clicking ‘More”, then ‘Save’. Figure 7 ROI manager menu for selecting and recording range of interest.
Open the spheroid image of the final timepoint (51 hours) with only red fluorescence protein (RFP) channel.
Click on the selected ROI from the same timepoint and click ‘Add’ to apply ROI to the spheroid image. Figure 8 Applying the gate for ROI manager into the image with red fluorescence of PI.

Select ‘Analyse’, then choose ‘Plot Profile’ to generate the plot between signal intensity and distance of the ROI. The axis title may differ from the figure, the axis can be adjusted by clicking ‘More’, then ‘Axis Options’. Figure 9 Analysis of the fluorescent profile using 'Plot Profile' menu.
To export the data of intensity profile, select ‘Data’, then ‘Copy All Data’.
Paste the data in GraphPad Prism to generate the plot from different timepoints. Different timepoints require at least three independent spheroids.
Perform the analysis in all timepoints from three different spheroids and plot them together in GraphPad Prism.
Strategy 2: Determination of cytotoxic activity using MFI of PI

From the MFI data ontained from step 4.1, percentage of specific killing of the PB-NK cells can be quantified using the following formula: The experimental MFI represents the mean fluorescence intensity of PI in the presence of effectors at a given E:T ratio, whereas the spontaneous MFI denotes the MFI of PI in wells containing only tumor spheroids to determine baseline spontaneous tumor cell death. Maximum MFI indicates the MFI of PI in wells with tumor spheroids treated with 0.1% Triton X-100 to ascertain the maximum level of cell death. This calculation provides a quantitative measure of the cytotoxic potential of PB-NK cells against the tumor spheroids.
Export the killing percentage data of every timepoints of three independent spheroids from all E:T ratios to GraphPad Prism.
Generate the plot between time (hour) and killing percentage to see NK cytotoxicity over time.

Strategy 3: Determination of overall cytotoxicity using area under the curve In this strategy, we use GraphPad Prism software to analyze the PI intensity data obtained from the previous part . Using this analysis, we can determine the overall killing activity of NK cells against different E:T ratios and different cancer types.
Import the data of PI intensity from the previous part to GraphPad Prism software. We recommend using the XY table format.
Generate a line graph showing the change in PI intensity over time.
Analyse the data by generating area under the curve (AUC). Figure 10 Analysis of area under the curve using Analysis menu in GraphPad Prism software.
Copy the total area displayed in the analysis panel and paste them in a new data table. The column graph should be seen in the graph area.
creator: Jakkrapatra Srisantitham
